vendors.name: Oracle Primavera P6
vendors.tagline: The enterprise scheduling standard on Bahrain government projects
vendors.market_position: The de-facto critical-path scheduling and project-controls tool on Bahrain’s large public and contractor projects, with an Oracle Bahrain-localised presence and a deep local training market.
pros: Large pool of P6-trained planners locally
cons: Enterprise pricing — overkill for small contractors
Scheduling only — not a full ERP
vendors.ba_specific_note: If you bid government or megaproject work in Bahrain, P6 schedules are often expected in the tender — this is the planning lingua franca, not an SME accounting tool.

vendors.name: Oracle Aconex
vendors.tagline: Cloud common-data-environment & document control
vendors.market_position: Oracle’s neutral common-data-environment for document control, RFIs and correspondence on large Bahrain projects — a Bahrain-localised Oracle product used where many parties must share a controlled record.
pros: Neutral CDE trusted on major projects
Strong audit trail for document control
cons: Needs process discipline to pay off
vendors.ba_specific_note: A document-control layer, not an invoicing system — pair it with an accounting/ERP tool. Most relevant on the Metro/causeway-scale projects in Bahrain’s pipeline.

vendors.name: Autodesk Construction Cloud
vendors.tagline: BIM + construction management, with a Manama reseller
vendors.market_position: The BIM-centred construction-management platform (design collaboration, model coordination, field management), served in Bahrain through a Manama reseller — the practical BIM must-have as projects mandate model-based delivery.
pros: Strong as BIM mandates spread in the Gulf
cons: Per-user costs add up across a team
Best value when you actually run BIM
Overlaps a PM tool you may already own
vendors.ba_specific_note: The BIM anchor for Bahrain. If your projects are heading toward model-based delivery, this is the reference platform — bought through the local reseller.

vendors.name: Bentley Systems
vendors.tagline: Infrastructure & civil engineering (STAAD, OpenRoads)
vendors.market_position: Engineering software for infrastructure and structures — STAAD, OpenRoads and the iTwin digital-twin stack — served in Bahrain via a Manama reseller, strongest on roads, structures and utilities work.
vendors.ba_specific_note: The pick for infrastructure and structural engineering specifically (causeway, metro, utilities) rather than general contracting or accounting.

vendors.name: Procore
vendors.tagline: Unified construction management — served from Dubai
vendors.market_position: A unified construction-management platform (project, quality, safety, financials) that reaches Bahrain from its Dubai MENA office. Capable and popular regionally, but with no confirmed Bahrain office or local reseller.
cons: No confirmed Bahrain footprint (serves from Dubai)
Support time zone is Dubai, not local
vendors.ba_specific_note: Genuinely capable, but you’ll be supported from Dubai, not Manama — confirm local implementation help before committing on a Bahrain project.

vendors.name: PlanRadar
vendors.tagline: Affordable site documentation, defects & snagging — public pricing
vendors.market_position: Field documentation, defect/snagging and QA/QC management that’s genuinely affordable for SME contractors, served across the Gulf from PlanRadar’s Dubai MENA hub — and one of the few with transparent per-user pricing.
pros: Quick to roll out on site
cons: Serves Bahrain from Dubai (no local office)
Field/QA focus — not a full ERP
vendors.ba_specific_note: The most SME-friendly entry point on this page — published pricing and fast setup make it easy to trial on one Bahrain project before scaling.

vendors.name: Focus Softnet
vendors.tagline: Regional cloud ERP with a registered Bahrain entity
vendors.market_position: A long-established Gulf ERP vendor with construction/contracting and real-estate modules, and — unusually for this list — a registered Manama entity (WLL) rather than remote-only coverage.
cons: Less specialised than pure contracting ERPs
vendors.ba_specific_note: One of the few vendors here with a real Bahrain legal entity — a point in its favour for local support and NBR-VAT familiarity.

vendors.name: PACT ERP
vendors.tagline: Contracting-specialised ERP (BOQ, cost control, procurement)
vendors.market_position: A construction/contracting-specialised ERP (BOQ, project cost control, subcontracts, procurement) served in Bahrain through a Manama partner — purpose-built for contractors rather than generic business ERP.
cons: Smaller brand than the global names
vendors.ba_specific_note: If you want contracting-specific ERP (not general accounting) with a local implementation partner, PACT is the focused option to shortlist.

vendors.name: Diamond Software (Catalyst)
vendors.tagline: Bahrain-native contracting ERP with job-costing & BOQ
vendors.market_position: The credible Bahrain-built option: a Manama-headquartered vendor whose Catalyst ERP targets construction and contracting with job-costing and BOQ, reporting 300+ customers — genuine local authenticity in a market otherwise served remotely.
pros: Local support in local time/VAT context
vendors.ba_specific_note: The local champion. In a market where most "Bahrain construction software" is sold from Dubai or India, Diamond is an actual homegrown product — worth a look for local support alone.

vendors.name: Zoho (Books)
vendors.tagline: Cloud accounting with a Bahrain (10% VAT) edition & public pricing
vendors.market_position: Cloud SMB accounting with a dedicated Bahrain edition handling 10% NBR VAT, and — rare on this page — fully published pricing. The natural accounting/invoicing layer for small Bahrain trades and contracting firms.
pros: Easy for small firms to adopt
cons: No e-invoicing mandate yet to certify against
vendors.ba_specific_note: The accounting anchor for Bahrain SMEs — cheap, VAT-correct and public-priced. Run it alongside a site/PM tool rather than expecting it to manage projects.

vendors.name: TallyPrime
vendors.tagline: Widely-used SMB accounting, VAT-ready for Bahrain
vendors.market_position: The on-premise/SMB accounting workhorse widely used across the Gulf, "VAT-ready for Bahrain" via local Gold Partners — familiar to many Bahrain accountants and bookkeepers, with published licence pricing.
pros: Very widely used by Gulf SMBs/accountants
vendors.ba_specific_note: If your accountant already runs Tally, staying with it for books is the path of least resistance — just don’t expect it to run the project side.

vendors.name: Wafeq
vendors.tagline: Arabic-native cloud accounting + e-invoicing readiness
vendors.market_position: Arabic-first cloud accounting with a Bahrain edition and e-invoicing pedigree from the wider GCC — a forward-looking accounting pick if/when NBR introduces an e-invoicing mandate.
cons: Pricing not public on the BH edition
No BH e-invoicing mandate to certify against yet
vendors.ba_specific_note: Bahrain has no e-invoicing mandate yet — but if you want an Arabic-native book that’s already e-invoicing-minded, Wafeq is a sensible hedge.

vendors.name: National Bureau for Revenue (NBR)
vendors.tagline: Bahrain VAT (10%) — the highest standard rate in the GCC
vendors.market_position: Bahrain’s tax authority administers VAT at 10% — raised from 5% on 1 January 2022, the highest standard VAT rate in the GCC. Registration is mandatory above BHD 37,500 of annual supplies (voluntary above BHD 18,750).
vendors.ba_specific_note: Make sure any accounting tool is set to Bahrain’s 10% rate (not the GCC-typical 5%). Unlike Saudi/UAE, there is no e-invoicing mandate to certify against — yet.

vendors.name: CRPEP (Engineering Professions Regulator)
vendors.tagline: Mandatory licensing for engineers & engineering offices
vendors.market_position: The Council for Regulating the Practice of Engineering Professions (Law No. 51 of 2014) licenses every engineer and engineering office in Bahrain — and only CRPEP-licensed offices can submit building-permit applications through Benayat.
pros: The legal gate to practise engineering
Required to lodge Benayat permit applications
vendors.ba_specific_note: If you run an engineering office in Bahrain, CRPEP licensing is non-negotiable — and it’s the credential that lets you file permits through Benayat.

vendors.name: Ministry of Works Contractor Classification
vendors.tagline: National contractor grading for public works
vendors.market_position: The Ministry of Works classifies contractors by category and grade (AA highest, then A, B…) under Ministerial Decree No. 9 of 2017 — the grading that determines which government works you can bid, with final listing via the Tender Board.
cons: Grade caps the work you can bid
Not required for purely private work
vendors.ba_specific_note: Bahrain’s analogue to Saudi/Gulf contractor classification — your grade is your ceiling on government tenders, so clean audited books (your ERP) help you grade up.

vendors.name: Bahrain Tender Board
vendors.tagline: The gateway to all government procurement
vendors.market_position: The independent regulator of all Bahrain government procurement. Suppliers and contractors must register to bid, and tenders at or above BD 10,000 are posted on its eTendering portal.
vendors.ba_specific_note: If public-sector work is your market, Tender Board registration plus the right MoW classification are the two keys — track both in your compliance calendar.

vendors.name: LMRA & Bahrainisation
vendors.tagline: Expat work permits + workforce-nationalisation quotas
vendors.market_position: The Labour Market Regulatory Authority issues and controls expatriate work permits and enforces Bahrainisation (national-workforce) quotas — directly shaping how a contractor staffs and prices a job.
vendors.ba_specific_note: Workforce mix isn’t just HR in Bahrain — LMRA permits and Bahrainisation quotas feed directly into your labour cost and your ability to win/renew.

vendors.name: Benayat (Building Permits)
vendors.tagline: The official building-permit e-system
vendors.market_position: Bahrain’s official online building-permit system (Ministry of Municipalities Affairs and Agriculture). Permits are mandatory, applications are lodged digitally — only by CRPEP-licensed offices — and fire-safety sign-off is built into the flow.
vendors.ba_specific_note: Build the Benayat permit (and the CRPEP-office requirement to file it) into every Bahrain project programme — it’s the gate to breaking ground.

vendors.name: Civil Defence (Fire Safety)
vendors.tagline: Mandatory fire-safety approval in the permit process
vendors.market_position: The General Directorate of Civil Defence (Ministry of Interior) enforces fire-safety requirements as part of the building-permit process, based on NFPA standards and the Civil Defence Law — fire approval is mandatory for buildings.
vendors.ba_specific_note: Treat fire-safety (Civil Defence/NFPA) as a design input from day one — retrofitting compliance late is the expensive way to learn it’s mandatory.

vendors.name: Bahrain Kuwait Insurance Company (BKIC)
vendors.tagline: Bahrain’s market leader for engineering & contractor cover
vendors.market_position: The leading insurer in Bahrain by gross premium since 2008 and a GIG (Gulf Insurance Group / Fairfax) operating company — the former "GIG Bahrain" site now redirects to BKIC. Rated A- (Excellent) by AM Best, and uniquely licensed in both Bahrain and Kuwait. Writes construction/erection all risks, contractor plant & machinery and professional indemnity.
pros: Construction/erection all risks + contractor plant & machinery + PI
GIG/Fairfax balance sheet for large-risk capacity; cross-border BH+KW
cons: Engineering detail thin on the public site
vendors.typical_pricing: Quote-based / risk-rated (not public) — standard for Gulf engineering cover
vendors.ba_specific_note: This is effectively "GIG Bahrain" (same company) — the default choice for sizeable Bahrain contractor and engineering risks; engage a broker for CAR/PI quotes.

vendors.name: Solidarity Bahrain
vendors.tagline: The consolidating Takaful insurer, now with Bahrain National Insurance’s engineering book
vendors.market_position: A Central Bank of Bahrain-regulated Takaful (Shariah-compliant) insurer that completed its 100% acquisition of Bahrain National Insurance (BNI) in April 2025, roughly doubling its scale and absorbing BNI’s engineering/marine/general-accident lines. Offers business cover spanning fire, marine, liability and engineering.
cons: BNI integration still bedding in (2025 deal); CAR not surfaced as a clear public product
vendors.typical_pricing: Quote-based / risk-rated (not public) — standard for Gulf engineering cover
vendors.ba_specific_note: Replaces "Bahrain National Insurance" as a standalone listing — BNI is now Solidarity. The natural Takaful counterpart to BKIC for contractor cover.

vendors.name: GIG Takaful (Takaful International)
vendors.tagline: Bahrain’s pioneer Shariah-compliant CAR & engineering cover
vendors.market_position: Established 1989 as the first Islamic insurer in Bahrain, now a GIG Bahrain subsidiary (branded GIG Takaful) with a GIG-group A- (AM Best) association. Its Contractors All Risks product indemnifies against sudden physical loss/damage to contract works and extends to third-party liability during the construction period.
pros: Confirmed CAR product extending to construction-period third-party liability
Sharia-compliant for contractors who require it
Backed by GIG Bahrain / Gulf Insurance Group
cons: Smaller standalone scale than parent BKIC
Effectively the Takaful arm of the same GIG group as BKIC (overlap)
vendors.typical_pricing: Quote-based / risk-rated (not public) — standard for Gulf engineering cover
vendors.ba_specific_note: Same group as BKIC — the Takaful alternative rather than a fully independent third option. Appears as both "Takaful International" and "GIG Takaful."

vendors.name: ProTenders
vendors.tagline: Construction project intelligence + eTendering — find projects early, with decision-maker contacts
vendors.market_position: A UAE-born, pan-GCC construction-intelligence and eTendering platform used by developers, consultants, contractors and suppliers to track upcoming/ongoing projects and respond to tenders. This is genuine B2B construction lead-gen — finding and pitching real project work — not a residential home-services app.
pros: Purpose-built for construction (real contractor lead-gen, not residential)
Tracks projects in planning/design/tender/under-construction with decision-maker contacts
Free company-profile tier for visibility; integrated eTendering
cons: Opaque, quote/demo-only pricing for the intelligence modules
Real value is paywalled behind a sales gate
vendors.typical_pricing: Quote/demo-based (free "Company Profiles" tier; intelligence + eTendering custom-priced)
vendors.ba_specific_note: The only genuine construction lead-gen reaching Bahrain — its pan-GCC project intelligence covers the Bahrain pipeline. There is no Bahrain-native construction lead-gen platform; local home-services apps (ServiceMarket, HomeX, Homefix) are residential, not construction-contractor.
